resumeWriting.eyebrow: Your resume isn't getting callbacks. Let's fix that.
resumeWriting.h1: A Resume That Actually Shows What You've Done
resumeWriting.subtitle: Stop listing responsibilities. Start showing results.
resumeWriting.supporting: Your current resume probably reads like a job description. 'Responsible for...' 'Managed...' 'Worked on...' That's what everyone writes. And that's why everyone gets ignored. We rebuild your resume to show the actual impact you've had — the problems you solved, the results you delivered, the scale you operated at.
highlights: Clear demonstration of your actual level
Format that works for both ATS and humans

heroModules.title: Find your wins
heroModules.body: We dig through your experience to find the real impact.

heroModules.title: Rebuild from scratch
heroModules.body: Every bullet gets rewritten for results and scope.

heroModules.title: Polish and refine
heroModules.body: One revision round to make sure it's perfect.

snapshot.title: What changes after a resume rebuild
snapshot.subtitle: Your experience doesn't change. How you present it does.

items.title: Your level is obvious

items.question: How is this different from a resume template?
items.answer: We don't give you a template. We rebuild your content from scratch, focused on your specific experience and target roles.

linkedinOptimization.eyebrow: Recruiters aren't reaching out. Let's change that.
linkedinOptimization.h1: A LinkedIn Profile That Makes Recruiters Come to You
linkedinOptimization.subtitle: Stop being invisible. Start getting inbound.
linkedinOptimization.supporting: Your LinkedIn profile is probably working against you. Generic headline. Vague summary. Experience that reads like your resume (which is also probably not great). Recruiters scroll past thousands of profiles. We make sure they stop at yours.

interviewPrep.eyebrow: You're getting interviews. Now let's win them.
interviewPrep.subtitle: Stop winging it. Start closing.
interviewPrep.supporting: Getting interviews isn't your problem. Converting them is. You walk in hoping for the best, ramble through behavioral questions, and leave wondering what you should have said. That's fixable. We build your story bank, practice the hard questions, and make sure you walk in ready.

salaryNegotiation.eyebrow: They made an offer. Don't leave money on the table.
salaryNegotiation.h1: Salary Negotiation That Gets You Paid What You're Worth
salaryNegotiation.subtitle: Most people take the first number. You won't.
salaryNegotiation.supporting: You got the offer. Congratulations. Now comes the part most people mess up. They feel grateful, uncomfortable, or unsure — and they accept whatever's offered. That's tens of thousands of dollars left on the table. We help you understand your leverage, prepare your ask, and negotiate with confidence.

heroModules.title: Strategy
heroModules.body: Know what to ask for and how.

items.title: You know your worth
items.body: Data-backed understanding of your market value.

items.title: You have a plan
items.body: Not just a number, but a conversation strategy.

items.title: You can ask
items.body: You've practiced. You know what to say.

mistakes.title: Why most people leave money on the table
mistakes.subtitle: These are avoidable. We help you avoid them.

items.title: Accepting too fast
items.body: Feeling grateful and saying yes immediately.

items.title: Not asking at all
items.body: Assuming the offer is final or you'll seem greedy.

items.title: Going in blind
items.body: Not knowing your market value or what to ask for.

heroCard.title: What you get

items.title: Practice the conversation

items.question: Can I really negotiate?
items.answer: In most cases, yes. Companies expect it. We help you do it professionally.

items.question: What if they rescind the offer?
items.answer: Extremely rare when done professionally. We help you approach it right.

items.question: What about equity and bonus?
items.answer: We look at total comp — base, equity, bonus, signing, and benefits.

items.question: How much can I actually negotiate?
items.answer: Depends on your leverage and the situation. We help you figure out what's realistic.

offerStrategy.eyebrow: You have options. Let's choose wisely.
offerStrategy.subtitle: Compare offers, evaluate trade-offs, and make the right choice.
offerStrategy.supporting: Multiple offers? Congratulations — that's a good problem to have. But it's still a problem. Which one is actually better? How do you compare comp when the structures are different? What about growth, culture, and fit? We help you evaluate offers holistically and make a decision you'll feel good about.

heroModules.title: Decision framework
heroModules.body: Make a choice you'll feel good about.

items.title: Accurate comparison
items.body: You understand what each offer is actually worth.

items.title: Trade-offs understood
items.body: You know what you're gaining and giving up with each option.

items.title: Choice you feel good about
items.body: You make a decision with eyes open.

mistakes.title: How people mess up the choice
mistakes.subtitle: We help you avoid these.

items.title: Comparing base only
items.body: Ignoring equity, bonus, and benefits.

items.title: Ignoring growth
items.body: Taking higher comp at a place that will stall your career.

items.title: Overthinking
items.body: Getting paralyzed by too many factors.

items.title: Accurate math
items.body: We compare comp the right way — total, not just base.

items.title: Holistic view
items.body: Money matters, but so does growth and fit.

items.title: Outside perspective
items.body: Sometimes you need someone not emotionally invested to see clearly.

items.title: What's it worth?
items.body: Total comp, correctly calculated.

items.title: Where does it lead?
items.body: Career trajectory and opportunity.

items.title: Will you thrive?
items.body: Culture, team, and work itself.

items.title: Share the offers
items.body: We review what you've received.

items.title: Analyze together
items.body: We compare comp and evaluate trade-offs.

items.body: You make a choice you feel confident about.

items.question: How do you compare equity?
items.answer: We look at strike price, vesting, company stage, and realistic liquidation scenarios.

items.question: What if the offers are very different?
items.answer: That's exactly when this helps most. We build a framework for comparison.

items.question: Can you help me negotiate first?
items.answer: Yes. Many clients negotiate with our help, then use this to decide.

items.question: What about factors like location?
items.answer: We factor in cost of living, remote policies, and location preferences.
